content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA consisting of NNN non-negative integers.

You need to perform the following operation (N−1)(N - 1)(N−1) times:


Sort the array AAA in ascending order.
Let MMM be the current size of the array AAA. Replace the array AAA by [(A2−A1),(A3−A2),…,(AM−AM−1)][(A_2 - A_1), (A_3 - A_2), \ldots ,(A_M - A_{M-1})][(A2​−A1​),(A3​−A2​),…,(AM​−AM−1​)]. Note that each time the operation is performed, the size of the array reduces by one.
Find the remaining element after (N−1)(N-1)(N−1) operations.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT denoting number of test cases. The TTT test cases then follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the size of the array.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, output single line: the remaining element after (N−1)(N-1)(N−1) operations.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: The operations look like:

Operation 111: Sort the array. Now, AAA becomes [0,3,19,29][0, 3, 19, 29][0,3,19,29]. Replace the array. Thus, A=[(3−0),(19−3),(29−19)]=[3,16,10]A = [(3-0), (19-3), (29-19)] = [3, 16, 10]A=[(3−0),(19−3),(29−19)]=[3,16,10].
Operation 222: Sort the array. Now, AAA becomes [3,10,16][3, 10, 16][3,10,16]. Replace the array. Thus, A=[(10−3),(16−10)]=[7,6]A = [(10-3), (16-10)] = [7, 6]A=[(10−3),(16−10)]=[7,6].
Operation 333: Sort the array. Now, AAA becomes [6,7][6, 7][6,7]. Replace the array. Thus, A=[(7−6)]=[1]A = [(7-6)] = [1]A=[(7−6)]=[1].



Thus, the remaining element after 333 operations is 111.

Test case 2: Since N=1N = 1N=1, you don't need to perform any operation. The remaining element after 000 operations is 777777777.
